heading: Public safety Interoperability Board
label: 1423 Public safety Interoperability Board
notes: Amendments2022—Subsec. (e). substituted “chapter 10 of title 5” for “FACA” in heading and “Chapter 10 of title 5” for “The Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.)” in text.Pub. L. 117–286
amendments: Amendments2022—Subsec. (e). substituted “chapter 10 of title 5” for “FACA” in heading and “Chapter 10 of title 5” for “The Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.)” in text.Pub. L. 117–286

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is established within the Commission an advisory board to be known as the “Technical Advisory Board for First Responder Interoperability”.

children.label: I
children.text: 2 members shall be representatives of national wireless providers;

children.label: II
children.text: 1 member shall be a representative of regional wireless providers; and

children.label: III
children.text: 1 member shall be a representative of rural wireless providers;

children.label: ii
children.text: 3 members shall be representatives of equipment manufacturers;

children.label: I
children.text: not less than 1 member shall be a representative of management level employees of public safety entities; and

children.label: II
children.text: not less than 1 member shall be a representative of employees of public safety entities;

children.label: iv
children.text: 3 members shall be representatives of State and local governments, chosen to reflect geographic and population density differences across the United States; and

children.label: v
children.text: all members shall have specific expertise necessary to developing technical requirements under this section, such as technical expertise, public safety communications expertise, and commercial network experience.

children.heading: Non-voting member
children.label: B Non-voting member
children.text: The Assistant Secretary shall appoint 1 non-voting member to the Interoperability Board.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), members of the Interoperability Board shall be appointed for the life of the Interoperability Board.

children.heading: Removal for cause
children.label: B Removal for cause
children.text: A member of the Interoperability Board may be removed for cause upon the determination of the Chairman of the Commission.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: 3 Vacancies
children.text: Any vacancy in the Interoperability Board shall not affect the powers of the Interoperability Board, and shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment.

children.heading: Chairperson and Vice Chairperson
children.label: 4 Chairperson and Vice Chairperson
children.text: The Interoperability Board shall select a Chairperson and Vice Chairperson from among the members of the Interoperability Board.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: 5 Quorum
children.text: A majority of the members of the Interoperability Board shall constitute a quorum.

children.heading: Duties of the Interoperability Board
children.label: c Duties of the Interoperability Board
A
children.text: develop recommended minimum technical requirements to ensure a nationwide level of interoperability for the nationwide public safety broadband network; and

children.label: B
children.text: submit to the Commission for review in accordance with paragraph (3) recommended minimum technical requirements described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Consideration
children.label: 2 Consideration
children.text: In developing recommended minimum technical requirements under paragraph (1), the Interoperability Board shall base the recommended minimum technical requirements on the commercial standards for Long Term Evolution (LTE) service.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which the Interoperability Board submits recommended minimum technical requirements under paragraph (1)(B), the Commission shall approve the recommendations, with any revisions it deems necessary, and transmit such recommendations to the First Responder Network Authority.

children.heading: Review
children.label: B Review
children.text: Any actions taken under subparagraph (A) shall not be reviewable as a final agency action.

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: d Travel expenses
children.text: The members of the Interoperability Board shall be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, at rates authorized for employees of agencies under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5, while away from their homes or regular places of business in the performance of services for the Interoperability Board.

children.heading: Exemption from chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: e Exemption from chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 shall not apply to the Interoperability Board.

children.heading: Termination of authority
children.label: f Termination of authority
children.text: The Interoperability Board shall terminate 15 days after the date on which the Commission transmits the recommendations to the First Responder Network Authority under subsection (c)(3)(A).
